text: His claim was that if anyone actually put the effort they could make something better than this. Seeing as how this is just random loops and discolorations for the most part, that shouldn't be hard. Nightbyer isn't claiming to be a good YTPer, he's saying that this video shows an extreme lack of effort.
author: @ntuninerlo

text: Bruh, fast-paced YTPs requires more efforts since you're executing jokes and gags in short amount of time to make your YTPs longer than it's original length so consider it as experimental. Also, it depends how much edits you can cramped in on your editing software. They're mostly relying on fast-paced stutter loops and scramblings accompanied with buzz loops, pitch shifting and flashing colors with various effects thrown in for the visual messups this time around.
author: @KennyPHClassics

text: ​ @KennyPHClassics  thanks for replying to my 10 year old comment lol. i have grown since then. you are certainly allowed to have your preference and this YTer did a good job. sorry for leaving this negativity sitting here for 10 years
author: @ntuninerlo

text:
@ntuninerlo  It's okay, since everybody saying these fast-paced YTPs are BAD, but the biggest problem that they didn't realize that they put so much efforts to get this style of editing. YTPs are more advanced than the usual regular editing, since you're manipulating it's dialogues and scenes to make a shocking piece of entertainment. Sometimes they're funny, and sometimes they're not, but it depends on your taste.

Usually, I love to combine fast-pacing and slow-pacing to my YTPs to make them watchable.
author: @KennyPHClassics

text: I'm going to guess that you're new at this whole thing, what with how late you are. Some people are into this kind of poop, I get it. But to say that this took any amount of skill to make shows a *complete lack of understanding on your part*. This kind of thing takes no imagination and almost no effort. If you're into it, you're into it, but don't try to tell me that it's good.
author: @nightbyer

text:
@TheKray11 That's exactly what I'm _n o t_ saying. To sum up what I said: I like to see poops that show creativity and real effort. I don't know how much effort the poster of this video put into it, but it's horribly uncreative.

Some people will respond saying that poops aren't creative and just exploit memes. Sure, I'll agree with that but to an extent. Just because the source material is the same doesn't mean the final product can't be vastly different.

text: No, it's great this is what they've become. Grow up.
